entry.title: Re: Develop ALV report based on user input
entry.content:
Dear Mahesh

The way you displayed in image , ALV may not support.  You need to develop a Module Pool Screen, There you put side by side the Target and achieved and you need to rearrange the information.  

First you design it in a spreadsheet and consult with your functional consultant and design the module screen.

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Re: Trigger an outbound Idoc when a PR is created.

We keep getting this error when we try to create an order/sales document with seven difrent spesific material numbers.

Does anyone know the reson for this?

-something with the material master data?

-something with the type of order created/sales order created?

 

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Configure print service entitlements
entry.content:
Hello @antoniomasseretti,
You can check if you are a Global Account Admin by following the steps:
Go to the Global Account –> Security –> Role Collections and than Global Admin.
All Users which have Global Admin rights will be displayed.

Br,
Manoel

entry.title: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Directory - Differences
entry.content:
Hello All,

What is the difference between SAP Identity Authentication and Local Identity Directory in SAP IPS/IAS ?

In both cases, users are stored in SAP IAS, but I don't understand the difference and when each solution should be used.

Thank you very much!

 

BEst Regards

entry.title: Re: How to enable the assessable value field for Non Indi...
entry.content:
Hi 

If you want enable it in MIRO 

so see this thread 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/unable-to-edit-assessable-value-during-custom-miro-on-sap-s4-hana-cloud/qaq-p/12750033

Aslo see this if it is for UAE 

I dont know for which country you are using VAT. As per my knowledge it is in GULF now . It is UAE so see below thread  Point 8 and 9https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2121275fa0824198b65bf1f4d71e5347/9775efea325d4c9fbb5d4c17...

8 TAE1 line item appears, and Assessable value field is enabled.

9 Assessable value field appears only for United Arab Emirates import scenario and only if the TAE1 condition type is classified as Customs Duty condition.

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: Title: ErrorMessage: App could not be opened because the SAP UI5 component of the application could
Re: SAP S/4Hana
entry.content:
Welcome @Kenneth525 to SAP Community!

I'd like to share some tips with you for getting started:

  • SAP Managed Tags (subscribe to your topic areas of interest)
  • Products and Technology boards
  • Topic pages (get to know about SAP products)
  • Blog posts (read what other community members have to say)
  • Discussion groups (discuss, connect, share) - join as many public groups as you like
  • Discover the SAP Community - How-To Video Series from my colleague @sveabecker

entry.title: S4 Employee BP Integration: Adding Employment Role to existing Business Partner with Vendor Role
entry.content:
Hey there, we are within a brown field conversion and implemented the New Data Model for employees. 

The employee BP is in NR HR0XXXXXX external (PERNR integrated), the employment BP war planend for number range 99 99000000. This is the grouping for the travel vendors. Those were synced to BPs (same number before the conversion)

Now the problem: if we run the sync report it generates a new BP 99... but there is already one with the PERNR in LFB1. In my opinion this link is perfect for matching them? In VMODE1 we did not have a problem. 

What can we do???

entry.title: error while installing SAP ABAP netweaver application server for Learning
entry.content:
Hello , 

I followed this guide to install SAP Netweaver to start practising , after updating the License and unraring again all the 11 parts of SAP Abap Netweaver, I stopped having this error (Step returns modlib.jslib.caughtException)

Now , that's the error that I have :

terminate called after throwing an instance of 'EThreadException'

ThrMtxUnlock failed: Operating system error occurred.
/bas/749_REL/bc_749_REL/src/ins/SAPINST/impl/src/selfextract/iauxsysex.c:387: child /tmp/sapinst_exe.4895.1731072317/sapinst (pid 4896) has crashed.

Please help, I have been stuck for a long time 

#SAP, #Netweaver , #SAP local server @julieplummer20 @beyhan_meyrali

entry.title: Need inputs on how to change Field names while using class cl_salv_table
entry.content:
Hello,

Need inputs on below case:

In a report program I am using the class CL_SALV_TABLE to display final result of the internal table using ALV.

The structure which is used for the final output table has multiple fields and some of them pointing to same data element. Here is the example.

Final table name - gt_<table_name>

gt_<table_name> is TYPE TABLE OF ZP_S_<structure_name>.

the global structure ZP_S_<structure_name> has below fields:

component namecomponent typeshort description
<field1><field_type1>ID
<field2><field_type2>NAME
<field3><field_type3>Description
<field4><field_type3>Description
<field5><field_type3>Description

As field3,4,5 using same data element it is displaying the same field label in the ALV.

But I would like display valid/relevant field names which I provide in the program as field label.

In precise, now, after I execute report program output is as below:

IDNameDescriptionDescriptionDescription

I would like to display as shown below:

IDNameAddress Key 1Address Key 2Description

Could you please tell how to achieve this if I use same data element for more than one field?

Thanks!

entry.title: Risk category update on released documents
entry.content:
Hello,
After changing the risk category in credit master we are running the RVKRED09 but risk category is not getting updated to the delivery when credit status is "D". we need a solution so when risk category is changed still system can update the new risk category to the released deliveries and stop the further processing


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Risk category update on released documents
entry.content:
Hello,
 
The "Released documents are still unchecked" settings and the release validity is impacting whether RVKRED09 can recheck documents.
RVKRED09 always checks the release date in advance.
 
So if you try on the day of release or if you have OVA8 settings to extend the release validity, the delivery can't be selected by RVKRED09. Thus the risk category cannot be refreshed either.
 
See SAP Note 718260.
 
Report RFDKLI20 would still help in this case to update the risk category.
 
Or if the release is just valid for the given day, then modification from SAP Note 52637 could be implemented (manual change to FM).
 
Best regards,
Edina
SAP

entry.title: Re: IMPORT VAT will be calculated based on CIF and Basic...
entry.content:
I dont know for which country you are using VAT. As per my knowledge it is in GULF now . It is UAE so see below thread  Point 8 and 9https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/2121275fa0824198b65bf1f4d71e5347/9775efea325d4c9fbb5d4c17...

8 TAE1 line item appears, and Assessable value field is enabled.

9 Assessable value field appears only for United Arab Emirates import scenario and only if the TAE1 condition type is classified as Customs Duty condition.

See also these thread 

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/assessable-value-field-is-not-editable...

for India we have note 2475533 - GST IN: Assessable value field in MIRO is not editable

 

Regards

Rashid Khan

entry.title: SAP Build Apps – REST API Integration - Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL
entry.content:
SAP Build Apps – REST API Integration - Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL

In this blog, I will discuss how to customize where to fetch the CSRF token for SAP Build Apps classic REST API integration. In my previous blog  SAP Build Apps - OData Integration – Customize CSRF Token Fetching URL we already discussed how to customize where to fetch the CSRF token for OData integration. But if your app is still using classic REST API integration, you may follow the steps below to customize where to fetch the CSRF token.

For example, if your BTP destination ‘MyRESTDest’ setting is as shown below

 

#
#Thu Sep 05 07:22:58 UTC 2024
Type=HTTP
BuildApps.RequiresCsrf=true
Name=MyRESTDest
URL=https\://mycompany.com/REST/myrestsvc
ProxyType=Internet
AppgyverEnabled=true

 

You want to customize your CSRF token fetch to https://mycompany.com/REST/myrestsvc/path/svc1

You need to follow steps as below

1. Drag the HTTP Request flow function to the logic canvas to fetch the CSRF token. It can be triggered by the Page Mounted event or any other event that occurs before you perform any HTTP POST, PUT, PATCH or DELETE request to your REST API integration.

 

2. Set the following inputs:

  • URL: destinations/MyRESTDest/path/srv1
  • HTTP method: GET
  • Headers: { value: Fetch, header: x-csrf-token }

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

 

3. Assign the output outputs["HTTP request"].resHeaders["x-csrf-token"] to an app variable, e.g., CSRFToken.

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

4. Add an additional input to your REST API integration, for example, csrfToken with the field type set to Text.

Details are shown in the screenshot below:

5. Assign the additional input csrfToken to a new common request header named x-csrf-token with the formula query.additionalInputs.csrfToken.

Details are shown in the screenshots below:

6. At the flow function where you want to trigger an HTTP POST, PUT, or DELETE request to your REST API integration, assign the app variable (e.g., CSRFToken from step 3) to the additional input parameter ( e.g csrfToken from step 4)

Details are shown in the screenshots below:

entry.title: I want to Verify the Account at the time of account creation - Registration process in SAP CDC
entry.content:
Hi All ,

I am lookin for a policy or a way by which i can make the account which is created as a verified account

Is ther any another way to achieve this

Ruchita

entry.title: Re: can you use a CAL S4HANA with SAP BTC?
entry.content: Duplicated with https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/can-you-use-a-cal-s4hana-with-sap-btc/qaq-p/13931319

entry.title: Re: Need inputs on how to change Field names while using class cl_salv_table
entry.content:
You update the field catalog table again with your preferred name for that field.

Before you display the report,  loop over the field catalog table - and change the field display to your preferred name.

entry.title: Re: SAP IPS: Wrong value of schemas attribute
entry.content:
Hi  tskwin,

You can try steps below :

  1. This error suggests a mismatch or incorrect value in the “schemas” attribute. Double-check that the SCIM schema version aligns with SAC’s requirements and ensure you’re using the correct endpoint format.
    Enable detailed error tracing by setting the "ips.trace.failed.entity.content=true" property. This will give you visibility into the specific content or entities causing the failure, helping to identify schema violations or data inconsistencies more precisely.
  2. Start to test provisioning with a single user entity to minimize potential issues. Once it succeeds, you can incrementally test with more complex transformations.
    Revalidate and confirm each configuration step to ensure changes do not conflict with SAC’s SCIM API specifications.
  3. When reverting, ensure there is no residual transformation mappings or invalid configurations are left from the previous changes. Sometimes, lingering mappings may cause inconsistencies, even if the initial code worked fine.
    Make sure clear the cache or reset mappings in SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) if possible before reinserting the original code.
  4. This warning typically occurs when there are conflicting mappings or duplicate transformation paths in the SCIM configuration. Check your transformation rules and remove or correct any redundant mappings for target paths.
    Also, make sure your transformation logic aligns exactly with the SAC schema requirements to avoid conflicts.

Hope this helps ,thanks,

@Amin_Omidy

entry.title: Re: Type ERROR:Cannot read properties of undefined (readi...
entry.content: why don't u list your version here????

entry.title: Re: Production order : double confirmation B10 for the same personal number
entry.content: Hello Waebou, have you found a solution for that ? we're facing the same issue

entry.title: Re: 50% self life materials to be not considered for sale...
entry.content:
Hello 

In standard, MRP does not consider the batch expiration date while planning, so you won't find a solution in standard to automatically consider batches with 50% shelf life in MRP. 

I have seen companies implementing BAdIs to exclude expired batches from the MRP run, but I don't think they would work in this specific scenario where batches can be used for production but not for sales. 

Perhaps you need to use different MRP Areas for sales and production, and move the batches with less than 50% of shelf life to the production MRP Area.

Regards,
Caetano

entry.title: Re: Need inputs on how to change Field names while using...
entry.content:
It would be something like the following but it only refers to one field name and you would need to tailor the approach to your needs (kept short for brevity)...

DATA: alv_ref  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_table,
      cols_ref TYPE REF TO cl_salv_columns,
      col_ref  TYPE REF TO cl_salv_column.

* Create ALV here ...
...

cols_ref = alv_ref->get_columns( ).
TRY.
  col_ref = cols_ref->get_column( 'FIELD3' ).
  col_ref->set_medium_text( 'Some Text' ).
  CATCH cx_salv_not_found.
ENDTRY.

 

Regards,

Ryan Crosby

entry.title: Re: Issue with Derivation in a new lines BEx-Query (When...
entry.content:
Hi,

please also check note 2508938 and set parameter BICS_NEW_LINE_SID_NOT_EXIST = X in table RSADMIN.

Regards,

Gregor

entry.title: Ex ness Partner Code " ht0sopiioj " bonus Up to 90 % sign up 2024.
entry.content:
Ex ness Partner Code " ht0sopiioj " bonus Up to 90 % sign up 2024.
Why You Should Use the Ex ness Partner Code " ht0sopiioj " When Registering for a New Account

If you're planning to open a trading account with Ex ness, using a partner code can unlock a range of exclusive benefits, giving you an edge in the competitive world of online trading. One such Ex ness partner code free is "c_al7qaabx3o." In this article, we’ll explain how to add a partner code in Ex ness, its benefits, and why you should consider using it when you sign up for a new account.

Exclusive Bonuses and Promotions
A key advantage of using the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " is the access to exclusive bonuses and promotions. Ex ness frequently offers special bonuses to traders who sign up using a partner code. These could include deposit bonuses, reduced spreads, and other incentives that help you maximize your initial trading capital. By using the Ex ness partner code free, you get access to offers that help you boost your trading potential from the very beginning, making your start more profitable.

Dedicated Support and Assistance
When you use the Ex ness partner code free, you’re also eligible for personalized support. Often, Ex ness partner codes link you to a dedicated account manager or a specialized support team who understands your trading needs and preferences. This tailored support is invaluable, especially for beginners who may need assistance with platform navigation or strategy development. Having dedicated support can provide a smoother, more efficient trading experience, ensuring you're never alone on your trading journey.

Access to Exclusive Trading Tools
Another great reason to use the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " is the possibility of gaining access to exclusive trading tools. Ex ness offers a comprehensive set of tools designed to help traders refine their strategies and stay ahead of market trends. By using this partner code, you may unlock additional resources, such as advanced charting tools, market analysis, and trading signals. These exclusive tools give you a competitive edge when making trading decisions, ultimately optimizing your trades and increasing your profitability.

Improved Trading Conditions
One of the main concerns for new traders is often the cost of trading, including spreads, commissions, and other fees. By registering with the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " you might benefit from more favorable trading conditions, such as lower spreads or reduced fees. These improved conditions allow you to maximize your profits while minimizing the cost of each trade. This is especially important when you're starting out and want to make the most of every trade, without worrying about high transaction costs.

Enhanced Trust and Security
Ex ness is a trusted and regulated broker known for its strict security measures. When you sign up using the Ex ness partner code free "c_al7qaabx3o," you’re connecting with a reputable network that ensures your funds and personal information are secure. Ex ness implements high-level security protocols to protect traders, so you can focus on your trading strategies without worrying about data breaches or fraud. Using a verified partner code ensures that you’re working with a professional who follows best practices in the industry.

Join a Community of Successful Traders
By signing up with the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " you don’t just gain access to valuable tools and support—you also become part of a thriving community of successful traders. Many Ex ness partners offer exclusive access to private trading groups or forums where traders can share insights, strategies, and tips. Whether you're new to trading or an experienced trader, being part of such a community accelerates your learning and keeps you motivated on your trading journey.

Referral Rewards and Profit Sharing
In addition to the direct benefits, signing up with the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " could also open the door to referral rewards. Ex ness has a profit-sharing program that rewards partners for referring new traders. If you're an experienced trader, this is a great way to share your knowledge with others while earning additional rewards. It's a win-win: you help others succeed and earn extra income in the process.

Maximize Your Potential with Ex ness Partner Code Benefits
In addition to all the specific perks mentioned above, using the Ex ness Partner Code " ht0sopiioj " unlocks a combination of valuable opportunities that can significantly enhance your trading potential. From receiving personalized guidance and enjoying better trading conditions to gaining access to advanced tools and the support of a trading community, Ex ness Partner Code benefits are designed to provide you with a comprehensive advantage. These combined perks offer a holistic approach to improving your trading experience and positioning you for long-term success in the financial markets.

How to Add Partner Code in Ex ness
To get started, simply input the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " during the registration process when signing up for your Ex ness account. This ensures that you’ll be eligible for all the exclusive benefits, promotions, and trading tools associated with this partner code.

How to Change Partner Code on Ex ness
If you’ve already registered without a partner code or would like to change your current one, Ex ness allows you to change your partner code under certain conditions. You can easily contact Ex ness support to request the change, and they will guide you through the process.

Conclusion
Using the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " when registering for a new account offers numerous advantages that can significantly enhance your trading experience. From exclusive bonuses and better trading conditions to personalized support and access to premium tools, this partner code unlocks a world of benefits that can make a difference in your success as a trader.

Don’t miss out on the opportunity to kickstart your trading career with a competitive edge. Sign up today with the Ex ness partner code free " ht0sopiioj " and take full advantage of all these exciting benefits!

This article has provided a clear explanation of how to add a partner code in Ex ness and the advantages it offers. Sign up today and enjoy the perks that come with being part of the Ex ness community.

This additional paragraph emphasizes the broad scope of Ex ness Partner Code benefits, making the article more comprehensive and appealing while retaining the original structure and tone.

entry.title: Re: Capability of billing freight cost to customer in Embedded Transportation management in S/4HANA
entry.content:
We have batch split items in our outbound delivery, but we do the billing only on main item level.

Delivery:

Main item 10: 0 KG
Batch split 900001: 10 KG
Batch split 900002: 10 KG

Billing document:

Main item 10: 20 KG

With this scenario, TM does not pass the freight costs to SD billing document because we see that the cost distribution is based on the batch split item. For main item 10, there are only 0 kg.

Did anyone face this already?

entry.title: Re: MM Quotation with one line with zero price S/4 HANA 2022
entry.content: Hi, did you solve the problem? Similarly, I want to set 'complete' on an Rfq, for which I received 2 of 3 quotations; so I have one 'zero' quotation or a quotation in status 'to be deleted'. Thanks

entry.title: Managing Multiple Projects in Central Business Configuration (CBC) – A Quick Guide
entry.content:
Introduction

If you're working with SAP's Central Business Configuration (CBC) system and need to handle multiple projects (Evaluation and Implementation), this blog will help clarify how to manage them easily.

Scenario: Creating Multiple Projects in CBC

You may want to create both Evaluation and Implementation type projects in CBC and switch between them as needed. Here's a quick guide on how to do it and some limitations to keep in mind.

Key Points to Remember

  1. Multiple Projects, Multiple Tabs:

    • You can create multiple both Evaluation and Implementation type projects in CBC.
    • You can switch between these projects or open them in different browser tabs.

  2. Deployment Targets – Limited Number:

    • The number of deployment targets you can assign to your projects is limited depending on your system landscape (3-system or 2-system landscape setup).

Deployment Targets in a 3-System Landscape:

  • Evaluation Project:
    • 1 Starter Customizing Tenant
    • 1 Starter Development Tenant
  • Implementation Project:
    • 1 Customizing Tenant
    • 1 Development Tenant

In this case, you have 4 different tenants that can be assigned to your Evaluation and Implementation projects.

Deployment Targets in a 2-System Landscape:

  • Evaluation Project:
    • You can assign a deployment target to your Starter System.
  • Implementation Project (split into phases):
    • Scope and Organizational Structure Phase: Assign a deployment target to your Quality System.
    • Product-Specific Configuration Phase: Assign a deployment target to your Production System.

Who Can Create a Project?

  • Only Program Managers and Project Managers have the authorization to create new projects in CBC.
  • The user who creates the project is automatically assigned to it.

Important Notes:

  • You can manage multiple projects in CBC but remember that the number of deployment targets is limited based on your system landscape.
  • Make sure you're clear on which system (starter, customizing, development, quality, or production) needs to be assigned in each project phase.

Hope this helps clarify how to manage your projects in CBC!

entry.title: Re: Is bcp in SAP IQ compatible with bcp in SQL server?
entry.content: BCP uses the OpenClient interface. It essentially issues a 'select *' against the source. It does not take advantage of IQ native capability. IQ's native EXTRACT capability has more capability and will be faster.

entry.title: Re: GTS Table with Product Status
entry.content:
Hi Alison/Srinivas,

I marked a product for deletion in ecc and transferred it to GTS. Still in corsta table I'm not able to see any data related to product status prod3. But i can see that product in changed status . Is there any config needs to done for it to change to deleted ?

Thanks and Regards,

Aditya sasank.

entry.title: Dump while creating Down Payment from sales order for a particular account
entry.content:
When you choose "Create Down Payment", the system opens the input screen. We have filled this out, confirmed with OK and then a window opens with an error message.

This happens only for sales orders created for a particular account. 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Dump while creating Down Payment from sales order for...
entry.content:
Dear Customer, 

The reason the system is generating an exception is because a currency was not maintained for the Sales Organization inside the Sales Data tab of the Customer Account being used in the Sales Order.

Due to this, no pricing terms is created on the Down Payment request and therefore no instance for price and tax is generated.

You can refer to the KBA "3072252 - Exception When Creating Down Payment From Sales Order"

Best regards,

Arjun T H

SAP Product Support

entry.title: Save-the-date & Frühbucher-Spezial: SAP Financials Forum, 17.-19. März 2025
entry.content:
Hybrides SAP Financials Forum: 
17.-19. März 2025
Sichern Sie sich jetzt Ihren Frühbucherrabatt

Auch im kommenden Jahr haben Sie wieder die Möglichkeit, sich in kurzer Zeit einen intensiven Überblick über die aktuellen Top-Themen im Bereich SAP Financials zu verschaffen. 
 
Besuchen Sie unser SAP Financials Forum, welches vom 17.- 19. März 2025 in hybrider Form stattfindet. Kommen Sie zu uns nach Walldorf und nutzen Sie die Veranstaltung für Weiterbildung und Networking oder nehmen Sie ganz bequem online teil und genießen die Vorzüge eines virtuellen Events. 

Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit, Ihre individuelle Agenda aus den vorgesehenen Inhalten (Direkt-Link zu Agenda), bestehend aus mehreren parallel stattfindenden Vortragsblöcken zu erstellen:

  • 18 Schulungsvorträge: Die einzelnen Vortragsblöcke aus unterschiedlichen Themenbereichen wie Finance, Controlling, Cash & Treasury Management uvm., sind speziell als Schulungseinheiten mit theoretischer Hinführung, Systemdemo und Q&A-Sessions konzipiert.  

  • 2 Meet the Expert-Sessions: Im Anschluss an die Vorträge haben Sie die Möglichkeit, mit ausgewählten SAP-Experten zu diskutieren und Ihre spezifischen Fragen zu klären.  

  • 2 Kundeneinblicke: In kompakten Präsentationen erhalten Sie Praxiseinblicke von namhaften Kunden, die erfolgreich Projekte unter SAP S/4HANA Finance meistern und gemeistert haben. 

  • 2 Basic-Hands-On-Sessions: Werden Sie selbst aktiv! Nach einer theoretischen Einführung durch einen SAP-Experten haben Sie die Möglichkeit, an einem SAP Live-System Übungen zu ausgewählten Themen durchzuführen und anschließend Ihre Fragen zu diskutieren. Bitte beachten Sie den organisatorischen Hinweis am Ende dieser Mail.* 

  • Abendveranstaltung: Zum Abschluss des ersten Konferenztages bieten wir Ihnen ein Abendessen vor Ort an, bei dem sie einen Abend in entspannter Atmosphäre genießen und Ihr Netzwerk erweitern können. 

  • Führung durch SAP Experience Center: Zusätzlich haben Sie die Möglichkeit, an einer kostenlosen, hybriden Führung durch das SAP Experience Center teilzunehmen, in der verschiedene SAP-Lösungen zur nachhaltigen Wertschöpfung in einem End-to-End-Prozess veranschaulicht werden.*


Nicht verpassen: 
Buchen Sie Ihre Teilnahme bis 31. Dezember 2024 und erhalten Sie mit dem Kampagnencode WDEFIT_2025 einen Rabatt von 25% auf den Veranstaltungspreis.**  
 
Sie fragen sich, wie unser letztes hybrides SAP Financials Forum aussah? 
Lesen Sie diesen Blogpost mit Bildern vom letzten Event (März 2024).  
 
Für Rückfragen oder Anmerkungen wenden Sie sich gerne direkt an uns.

Jetzt anmelden: SAP Financials Forum

 

* Bitte beachten Sie, dass für die Hands-on Sessions und die Führung durch das SAP Experience Center nur eine begrenzte Anzahl an Plätzen verfügbar ist. Daher ist eine Teilnahme ausschließlich mit vorheriger, separater Registrierung möglich. Mehr Informationen hierzu erhalten Sie nach erfolgter Anmeldung zum Forum voraussichtlich ab Januar 2025.  
 
** Das Angebot ist nicht kumulierbar mit anderen Rabatten und Discountverträgen.

entry.title: Re: Custom domains for BTP CloudFoundry applications
entry.content: Thanks for sharing. The INFRA (with custom_domain plan) is an older version of Custom Domain Service. One can also choose custom-domain-manager (with standard plan). It has graphical user interface which might be easier for some.

entry.title: Re: Need advice/steps setting upTDE on ASE 16 with 70 dbs...
entry.content:
You can use one DEK for all databases.

Once encrypted, you do not have to decrypt a database.  Use 'transfer encryption key' (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE/e0d4539d39c34f52ae9ef822c2060077/ab8a5e079bae4204b0be90db9e115d06.html) to extract the master key and the DEKs from the source ASE and install them into the target/replica ASE so loads/replication work.

Chris

entry.title: Re: Transport the Initial Business Configuration Content...
entry.content:
Hi,

The "BC Initial Scope" is your very first configuration related transport. You need to move to T and P.

The term "CBC Staging transport request" is a general term for all the transport created due to changes in CBC, including initial scoping as well as CBC BC content upgrade.

Regards,

George

entry.title: Don't miss the opportunity and join our live sessions - Register now!
entry.content:
Unlock the expertise of top SAP professionals through live sessions and dive deeper into topics. 
Elevate your knowledge with specialized SAP courses and build valuable connections with peers and industry professionals.
Don't miss the opportunity and join our live sessions!

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Cloud Connector
    Europe time-friendly): November 26, 10:00 a.m.– 11:00 a.m. (CET)
  • Developing Apps with SAP Build Apps using Drag-and-Drop Simplicity
    (Europe time-friendly):     November 29, 8:00 a.m.– 10:00 a.m. (CET)

SAP ABAP Cloud

  • Getting Started With ABAP Cloud Development 
    (Asian-Pacific-Japan time-friendly): November 14, 11:00 p.m.–12:00 a.m. (SGT)
  • Stay certified - Back-End Developer - ABAP Cloud (Recording)


SAP S/4HANA

  • SAP S/4HANA Sales: Delivery and Transportation Scheduling
    (Asian-Pacific-Japan time-friendly): November 14, 11:00 p.m.–12:00 a.m. (SGT)
  • Migrating Data with the SAP S/4HANA Migration Cockpit in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    (Asian-Pacific-Japan time-friendly): November 21, 12:00 a.m.– 2:00 a.m. (SGT)

Please note

You can find the latest live sessions here to engage and learn with SAP experts and peers.


Never Miss an Event
Browse through the upcoming training events and benefit from the combination of expertise, experienced speakers, and the opportunity to network on innovative and current topics in the SAP world.
Browse more

entry.title: Re: Need advice/steps setting upTDE on ASE 16 with 70 dbs...
entry.content: Apologies. The writeup you are looking for is probably the 'Database Encryption' doc at https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE/833788dd3e9c413799014a0fd002d0b2/a6648bc9bc2b1014b48885ebe86c6f54.html

entry.title: Re: Efficient Management of CBC Type Transport Requests in S/4HANA Cloud Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

All tasks within CBC type transport requests are automatically released after implementation. The main/parent transport request will not move until you take ownership and release it. This automatic task release applies to CBC type transport requests.

Thanks!!!

Best Regards,

Guru Yog

entry.title: CSV data files - to work on IBP excercise
entry.content:
Hello,

From where I can download the data files to upload into master data types and KF while working on IBP exercises. 

Thanks

entry.title: SAP Datasphere connection with SAP Commerce Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

This is my first post, wish me luck ! 

I am looking for a way to connect SAP Datasphere # and  SAP Commerce Cloud #. We are planning to get data from SAP Commerce Cloud to SAP Datasphere and modeling at SAP Datasphere to report in SAP BusinessObjects WebI.

It seems like a two way to do it;

1-) Create a RESTful API and establish a connection

2-) Connect SAP Commerce Cloud to a MSSQL server and push the Commerce Cloud table data to it and update in a constant periods of time. Then connect the MSSQL server to SAP Datasphere.

Any advice for a third way or any comments on these two ways ?

The Best, Onur

entry.title: Re: Need advice/steps setting upTDE on ASE 16 with 70 dbs...
entry.content:
I would also recommend using a master key so automatic master key access can be configured for the ASEs so they start up with access to the encrypted databases. Otherwise encrypted databases cannot be accessed during recovery when the ASE server starts until a session is connected and unlocks the master key (step 2 below).
The steps to set up automatic master key access are:
1. Create/transfer master key
2. set encryption passwd '<master key password>' for key master
3. sp_configure "automatic master key access", 1
4. alter encryption key master with passwd '<password>' add encryption for automatic_startup
5. create/transfer DEK(s)
The encrypted master key (step 4) is stored by default at a location shown by 'sp_encryption mkey_startup_file, null' and can be changed before running step 4, but must be in a folder readable by the ASE when it starts. 

Chris

Regards

Virinchy

entry.title: Re: How to upload the bulk data in S/4HANA Public Cloud?S...
entry.content:
Hi HEPSIBHA,

Unfortunately, with such a brief question, it is difficult to provide a great answer.

We would need to know more about your requirement. Are the Sales Orders / production orders coming from an external system via integration?

Specifically with Production Order, in most cases "production demand" (or Planned Independent Requirements (PIRs)) are derived from the creation of Sales Orders or Purchase Orders in the system. Later in the MRP run, the Planned Production Orders are created based on that demand tne the MRP rules set up in the system. However, Production Orders can also be created in the system directly or imported via api.

Many methods exist to bulk upload sales orders (and other objects) into the system.

Migration Cockpit

For one time uploads, usually and cut over before going live you can bring in open orders. This shouldn't be used for ongoing order imports. See the help for more detail including the available migration objects: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d5699934e7004d048c4801b552f3b013/f32db0c240484241abc53a876253e118.html?locale=en-US

Via API

For integrations with another system, typically via a Middleware like BTP Integration Suite, you can make use of Public APIs, see the Business Accelerator Hub 

For example: Sales Order A2X - There are many APIs there, for Sales, many of them are grouped in to the Communication Arrangement Sales Order Integration (SAP_COM_0109) (you can search SAP_COM_0109 in the API search). there are similar APIs covering Production with Production Planning Integration (SAP_COM_0104) and Manufacturing Execution - Order and Confirmation Integration (SAP_COM_0522). EDI is also an option.

Integration packages

Using the Public APIs, there are also predefined Integration Packages that can be used as accelerators: https://api.sap.com/products/SAPS4HANACloud/integrations/packages

Directly Via Available apps

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition also provides a number of apps for importing orders. For example: Import Sales Orders (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/fbf21ca2d6c1482c8473b7d76c0ed39d.html?locale=en-US).

You can explore all the available apps in the Fiori App Library as well. You can filter the list for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition apps.

I hope this helps, if you have more specific questions or can better describe your requirement, let us know.

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Re: Custom RAP Application Deployment to SAP S/4 HANA Pub...

entry.title: Re: Customer Checkout integration with VIVA

entry.title: How to implement cross container access between 2 multitenant (mtxs) CAP SAAS applications?
entry.content:
Hello CAP and HDI experts,

we have two CAP SAAS applications MDM and TPS, where we need the TPS HANA container to have read access to the MDM HANA container. Reason is performance optimisation with stored procedures, where a stored procedure in TPS container should have read access to views in the MDM container.

When a subscriber tenant in a BTP sub-account subscribes to the two SAA applications, the containers are created by the 2 saas-registrys and the schema content is created by the 2 service-managers of the applications.

The https://help.sap.com/docs/hana-cloud-database/sap-hana-cloud-sap-hana-database-developer-guide-for-cloud-foundry-multitarget-applications-sap-business-app-studio/using-synonyms-to-access-external-schemas-and-objects describes how to implement cross container access between to none multitenant HDI containers of type com.sap.xs.hdi-container. But with SAAS applications we use the 

service-manager which dynamically manages the containers of subscribing tenants behind the scene.
Can somebody provide the correct mta.yaml configuration for this scenario, if available? 
 
A similar request is described by https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/achieve-cross-hdi-communication-through-synonyms-using-mtx-sidecar/qaq-p/12567206
 
But there was no answer for this real life requirement.
 
Many thanks
Achim

entry.title: Re: Automatically rotate passwords on service accounts
entry.content: We have the same requirement to automatically rotate passwords on service accounts periodically, for ABAP and JAVA systems, and Oracle and HANA databases This also includes service/system accounts used in RFCs (SM59). This means the password manager would have to simultaneously update passwords in (at least): - SM59 connections - SU01 user master - Connecting systems (both internal systems and externally at partner firms) - we're talking about tens of thousands of service users in 500+ systems where each connection in each system uses it's own dedicated user, making it almost physically impossible to perform this manually. Is it possible to use a PAM (Privileged Access Management) solution or 3rd party tool for this requirement? Is there a SAP tool available to perform this function? SAP Business connector is mentioned, is any documentation concerning this topic available apart from https://support.sap.com/content/dam/support/en_us/library/ssp/products/connectors/bc/SBC_SecurityBestPracticesGuide_481.pdf?

entry.title: 🌐 𝐁𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐁𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬: 𝐀 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐭 𝐅𝐮𝐭𝐮𝐫𝐞 🌐
entry.content:
Join us on November 12th, 2024 for a 60-minute session that explores the 𝐒𝐮𝐩𝐩𝐥𝐲 𝐂𝐡𝐚𝐢𝐧 𝐑𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐥𝐢𝐞𝐧𝐜𝐞 𝐒𝐜𝐞𝐧𝐚𝐫𝐢𝐨 using 𝐒𝐀𝐏 𝐀𝐧𝐚𝐥𝐲𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐬 𝐂𝐥𝐨𝐮𝐝.

This event is part of the 𝐁𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐧𝐞𝐬𝐬 𝐁𝐮𝐢𝐥𝐝𝐞𝐫𝐬 platform, designed to help educators teach students how to craft data-driven stories and visualize key insights from complex business information.

👥 Why attend?

- Learn how to engage students with hands-on strategies for building resilient supply chains.
- Gain insights into using data visualization to address supply chain disruptions.
- Prepare students for the challenges of today’s data-driven world.

Register now and mark your calendars!

🔗👉 https://buff.ly/4gPcGW9  

hashtag#BusinessBuilders hashtag#SupplyChainResilience hashtag#SAPAnalyticsCloud hashtag#DataStorytelling hashtag#EdTech hashtag#HigherEd

entry.title: CSRF validation fails (403 Forbidden) from SAP Build Work Zone approuter
entry.content:
Hello Community,

The app contains a FileUploader element. The related backend oData service has CSRF protection enabled. To enable file upload, there is a controller extension for the FileUploader where the CSRF token is fetched and explicitly added (x-csrf-token) to the POST request header. It works correctly from SAP BAS with "Preview application".

The app is now deployed to Cloud Foundry, where it is added to a SAP Build Work Zone standard edition page. The upolad from the Build Work Zone site throws the error 403, and this is found in the approuter error log:

POST request to /sap/opu/odata/sap/Z<....> completed with status 403 The request contains an invalid x-csrf-token 

I can se in the browser debugger that the request does contain the x-csrf-token parameter with a plausible value. That part looks identical as when testing from SAP BAS where the token is accepted.

I have found note 3495019 "403 error when the AppRouter generates a CSRF Token" which seems relevant. Except I am pretty sure the CSRF protection is already disabled in the xs-app.json. Here it is:

{
  "welcomeFile": "/index.html",
  "authenticationMethod": "route",
  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/sap/(.*)$",
      "target": "/sap/$1",
      "destination": "<name of my Cloud Connector destination>",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    },
    {
      "source": "^/resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^/test-resources/(.*)$",
      "target": "/test-resources/$1",
      "authenticationType": "none",
      "destination": "ui5"
    },
    {
      "source": "^(.*)$",
      "target": "$1",
      "service": "html5-apps-repo-rt",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa"
    }
  ]
}

I did find number of similar questions, most notably:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/x-csrf-token-validation-success-from-standalone-app-but-fails-from-managed/qaq-p/12535042#M4699094

but no solved ones, or possibly I do not understand the recommendations and need more guidance.

Please advise what to do - thank you in advance!

entry.title: An IDoc without message type was passed to ALE Layer
entry.content:
Hello, 

After an client copy, from PROD to QAS, in QAS system embedded EWM doesn't send anymore messages to SAP ERP.  When I try to make an inbound delivery in EWM, all is good, but when I return to SAP ERP, the stock is with "On Order Stock".

In SMQ2, I have SYSFAIL with error message "An IDoc without message type was passed to ALE Layer" and Function Module is /SPE/INB_DELIVERY_CONFIRM_DEC.

Can someone help us?

I need to copy BOM #1 in alternative #5 & #6 (Sales and Costing)

There is a way to do this massively? I have like 1000 materials to do..

Thank you all

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Scripting (Part 2)
entry.content:
Hi Jan,

Thank you for sharing your perspective again. I understand that the use case might not be immediately clear to everyone. These scripts are specifically designed to address common challenges in post-SAP refresh activities, such as automated password updates and user management, which can be time-consuming and error-prone when done manually.

For customers dealing with frequent system refreshes, this automation has resulted in significant time savings and improved security, particularly when standard tools like eCATT have limitations. I’m always open to feedback and happy to provide more specific examples of the benefits realized by our clients if you’re interested.

Kind regards,
Mohammed

entry.title: How to delete a wrong entry from Organizational Unit?
entry.content:
I created a wrong entry in Organization entity. I want to delete it because it is wrong and should not be transported to the Production System.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to delete a wrong entry from Organizational Unit?
entry.content:
Please note that in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, once an organizational unit or assignment is activated, the system does not allow for the physical deletion or un-assignment of these entities. This is a design feature intended to maintain data integrity.

As an alternative, we recommend updating the description of any unwanted organizational units or assignments to "DO NOT USE" to indicate they should no longer be utilized.

For more information, please refer to the following KBA: 2857282 - Organizational Org Entity Deprecation - SAP for Me

If deleting a specific entry in sales area is critical to your business operations, we kindly advise you to coordinate with your SAP representative. They will be able to guide you through the necessary steps and discuss potential solutions based on your requirements.

entry.title: Material Groups Dependency
entry.content:
Hello All,

Our materials are organized into a three-tier category system: Group 1, Group 2, and Group 3. We are using fields MVKE-MVGR1-3, as this data is required in SD. We need dependencies across these categories, where the values in Group 2 are determined by the selections in Group 1, and the values in Group 3 depend on the selections in Group 2.

We’re unable to use the product hierarchy, as it is already designated for a different categorization.

Is there any standard solution to have this dependency?

Thanks,

entry.title: Re: Recap of SAP S/4HANA 2023 Highlights Webinar: Service
entry.content:
@VeeraRajachandrasekhar , SAP Fiori apps for maintenance management in the Service with Advanced Execution is probably what you are looking for. The "phase-based maintenance process" is tied to Fiori. As this is a forward-looking information, it may change without prior notice (as noted in the disclaimer). We update Road Map Explorer as we go, so please check back closer to the time. 

And @Raghunandan1 thank you for your follow-up comments. We are always looking to connect with customers and partners. To take the feedback actionable, we have a structured approach. If you or your customers are with RISE (SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition), perhaps Influence Opportunity - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, service management may be a good place. As you can see, it's rather lively and active. If you find product defect (bugs), then please use the standard support channel so that we can funnel your bug reports appropriately.

Thank you for your engagement.

entry.title: Re: Maintenance order printing getting time out
entry.content: Good day Yaswanth, I would recommend to create a ticket as Operations can validate the details for the 500 error (short dump). Make sure to provide a time stamp (incl. time zone indication), CB ID (user ID) and preferable steps to recreate the error. Component to select: XX-S4C-OPR-INC. Good luck! Esther

entry.title: Transitioning from no EBS to EBS Implementation
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm looking for a knowledge base article or guide to transition our company from having no Electronic Bank Statements to implementing EBS in SAP. In general, I understand we need to update our Bank Account Managment, review our bank data to match what GL accounts, and configure that correctly. I'm wondering if I'm missing something. 

Does anyone have an article or guide to make this significant change to EBS implementation?

entry.title: SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Updated Release Timeline and Maintenance Strategy
entry.content:
SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025

Updated Release Timeline and Maintenance Strategy

 

Updated release date for the BI 2025

Dear SAP BusinessObjects community,

We’re reaching out today to provide you with some important updates on the planned release of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025. As you know, SAP remains committed to ensuring the highest standards of quality and support for our BusinessObjects suite.

With that commitment in mind, we’ve made the decision to postpone the release of SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 from December 04th, 2024 to March 12th, 2025.

A reminder: Early Adopter Care program for SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025

To ensure BI 2025 meets the expectations and needs of our customers, with participants in our Early Adopter Care (EAC) program. Participants in the EAC program benefit from dedicated support from a project coach and the development team to address quickly critical issues, along with opportunities to share their expected future enhancements directly with the development team.

If you're interested in learning more about the program and the exciting capabilities of BI 2025, we encourage you to check out the latest details here : SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025 Early Adopter Care Program

 

Important updates to the SAP BusinessObjects BI support and maintenance strategy

In conjunction with the adjusted release date for BI 2025, SAP is introducing updates to the support and maintenance strategy . We believe these changes will provide clarity to our customers as we transition to BI 2025, helping them to make informed decisions about your deployment and upgrade strategies.

Here’s an overview of the upcoming changes:

1. End of support for SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.2

Please note that support for SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.2 will officially end on December, 31st 2024. For customers currently using BI 4.2, customers must upgrade to BI 4.3 or BI 2025 to ensure continued access to critical updates and .

2. Mainstream

To ensure a smooth transition to SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025, the mainstream maintenance for SAP BusinessObjects BI 4.3 is now extended by one additional year, until end of 2026.

3. New BI customer-specific maintenance

As part of our updated maintenance approach, we’re refining the types of support available during different phases of the product lifecycle:

- No more Priority 1 Support (P1) for BI 4.3 and BI 20xx releases: Moving forward, the P1 support classification will no longer be available.

- BI 4.3 and BI 20xx Mainstream Maintenance will be followed directly by Customer-Specific Maintenance (CSM). We will continue to deliver patches for the first year of Customer-Specific Maintenance (CSM) for vulnerabilities rated CVSS >= 7  impacting BI, as described in 3053725 - Security Corrections in Customer Specific Maintenance - SAP for Me.  

This approach will help ensure your system’s resilience against potential security risks, while aligning with SAP’s maintenance standards.

These adjustments align with SAP’s focus on supporting the security and stability of the SAP BusinessObjects BI platform during its lifecycle, even as we phase out older versions.

For detailed information of these changes and what they mean for your organization, please refer to our updated SAP BusinessObjects Maintenance Strategy 2024 document.

4.  Platform Availability Matrix for SAP BusinessObjects BI 2025

In order to help you to plan you future upgrade on the new version of SAP BusinessObjects, we will publish very soon the first version of the Product Availability Matrix for BI 2025, where you will find details about SAP software product versions, including availability and end of maintenance dates, upgrade paths, and technical release information (database platforms, Java platforms, operating systems, and so on).

 

Thank you for your interest and partnership

Thank you for being a part of our SAP BusinessObjects community. As always, please don’t hesitate to reach out to our team (mailto: sapaskanalytics@sap.com) with any questions or for further assistance.

Warm regards,

The SAP BusinessObjects Product Team

entry.title: Re: SAP Emarsys Integration with Whatapp
entry.content: Hi Hemanth, we have the same use case and I have some ideas, feel free to reach out to me at d.vondrovic@globus.cz .

entry.title: CDS View: How to provide the default value for hierarchy node variable in analytical queries
entry.content:
This blog is about – how to provide static or dynamic default value for hierarchy node variable in analytical queries.

Static Default values

Hierarchy node variable is defined using @AnalyticsDetails.variable annotation. Static default value for this variable can be provided using @AnalyticsDetails.variable.defaultHierarchyNode annotation.

In @AnalyticsDetails.variable annotation, selectionType need to set as #HIERRARCHY_NODE, also hierarchyBinding is specified along with referenceElement, MultipleSelections.

Example

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'Example of static default value'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
  serviceQuality: #X,
  sizeCategory: #S,
  dataClass: #MIXED
}
define transient view entity ZLR_RES_HIERNODE2
  provider contract analytical_query
  with parameters

    @EndUserText.label: 'Connection Hierarchy'
    p_hienm     : char5,

    @AnalyticsDetails.variable: {
       selectionType: #HIERARCHY_NODE,
       referenceElement: 'connid',
       hierarchyBinding: [{ type:#PARAMETER, value: 'p_hienm'}],
       multipleSelections: true,
       defaultHierarchyNode:
       {
         nodeType: 'carrid',
         node: [{element: 'carrid', value:'AZ' }]
       }

    }

    @Consumption.hidden: true
    p_hier_node : s_conn_id

  as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

  @AnalyticsDetails.query.displayHierarchy: #FILTER
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
  connid,

  currency,

  @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CURRENCY'
  @AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
  paymentsum


}
where
      currency = 'EUR'
  and connid   = $parameters.p_hier_node

 

Output

Dynamic Default Values 

Dynamic default values for hierarchy node variables should be derived from another CDS view (lookup view) at runtime.

@Consumption.derivation annotation enables derivation of the value for a parameter or a filter automatically at runtime by selecting a row from a given entity.

At first, a separate hierarchy node lookup view is defined.

Then hierarchy node variable is defined using @AnalyticsDetails.variable annotation.

To specify a default value for the previously defined node variable @Consumption.derivation.resultHierarchyNode is used.

@Consumption.derivation annotation contains,

lookupEntity, which provide the name of hierarchy node lookup view to get the hierarchy node.

resultHierachyNode annotation is used to provide hierarchy node value to a parameter.

resultHierarchyNode.nodeTypeElement must point to a field in the lookup entity and this field must return a fieldname. This fieldname (in the data of the lookup view) must be an existing fieldname in the lookup view.  If the value of field NodeTypeElement in the result of the lookup is initial, the row represents a leaf.

resultHierarchyNode.Mapping describes the mapping of the elements in the hierarchy view and the elements in the lookup entity.

Consumption.derivation.binding is possible to add filters to the lookup. This is generic and not specific to hierarchy nodes.

Example

Step 1: Defining Hierarchy node lookup view

Hierarchy node look up view is use as a lookup entity in the @Consumption.derivation annotation.

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_REQUIRED
@EndUserText.label: 'Derivation for node parameter-resultHierarchyNode'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
  serviceQuality: #X,
  sizeCategory: #S,
  dataClass: #MIXED
}
define view entity ZLR_RES_DERIVE_BASE as select from  t000
{
   cast( 'carrid1' as fieldname ) as nodeType,
   cast( 'LH' as s_carr_id ) as carrid1, 
   cast( ' ' as s_conn_id ) as connid1,
   cast( ' ' as rsshnodename  ) as nodename1,
   $session.user                as userName                                                                             
}
where mandt = '000'

 

Step 2: Defining hierarchy node variable and use Consumption.derivation annotation in analytical query

In this example, ZLR_RES_DERIVE_BASE view is used as lookupEntity- which provides default value to the parameter

 

@AbapCatalog.viewEnhancementCategory: [#NONE]
@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #NOT_ALLOWED
@EndUserText.label: 'Example of dynamic default value'
@Metadata.ignorePropagatedAnnotations: true
@ObjectModel.usageType:{
  serviceQuality: #X,
  sizeCategory: #S,
  dataClass: #MIXED
}
define transient view entity ZLR_RES_HIERNODE1 
provider contract analytical_query
with parameters

@EndUserText.label: 'Connection Hierarchy'
p_hienm : char5,

@AnalyticsDetails.variable: { 
   selectionType: #HIERARCHY_NODE, 
   referenceElement: 'connid',   
   hierarchyBinding: [{ type:#PARAMETER, value: 'p_hienm'}],
   multipleSelections: true
  
    }
  
@Consumption.derivation: {
  lookupEntity: 'ZLR_RES_DERIVE_BASE',
  resultHierarchyNode: {
      nodeTypeElement: 'nodetype',
      mapping: [
            { hierarchyElement: 'carrid', lookupElement: 'carrid1' },
            { hierarchyElement: 'connid', lookupElement: 'connid1' },
            { hierarchyElement: 'nodename', lookupElement: 'nodename1' }
      ]
    }
}
@Consumption.hidden: true
 p_hier_node : s_conn_id

as projection on ZOQ_FLIGHT
{

@AnalyticsDetails.query.displayHierarchy: #FILTER
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS
@AnalyticsDetails.query.totals: #SHOW
connid,

currency,

@Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CURRENCY'
@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #COLUMNS
paymentsum
 
}
where currency = 'EUR' and connid = $parameters.p_hier_node

 

Output

Please note that in the older release instead of using @AnalyticsDetails.variable annotation, @Consumption.hierarchyNodeSelection is used.

@Consumption.hierarchyNodeSelection: { 

  hierarchyElement: 'connid',

  hierarchyBinding: [{ type:#PARAMETER, value: 'p_hienm'}],

  multipleSelections: true

}

entry.title: Re: Formatting error when printing from an ERP to Zebra L...
entry.content:
Open Control Panel and look at Programs and Features.

Look for Crystal Reports for Visual Studio, it should show the version number.

What is version is Sage using?

Open a report and click on File, Page Setup and look at the default printer, use the Dissociate checked on is a must.

In current versions the Printer dialog box show the saved version of the printer used:

See this Blog I wrote for dealing with Custom Paper sizes:

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/printing-crystal-reports-custom-paper-sizes/ba-p/13396188

If you are using a newer version of the runtime Crystal will look for the Name of your custom paper size, all you have to do is create the custom paper name on each Zebra printer and CR will find it and use it.

By default when using the print button from the CR Viewer you can set the value to either PrintToPrinter or PrintOutputController, P2P uses the default printer properties so you must set the custom paper size as your default. POC allows you to use what is saved in the RPT file and the custom paper size.

Sage would need to allow you to select that feature, it is programmable. If they don't open each report, Click on Print and select the correct Printer and paper size and then click on Apply. Save the report, now when printing in Sage's app it should use those values and Orientation should not be an issue.

Contact Sage for support and how they suggest using their app.

entry.title: Contact and product data references in the Emarsys scripting language
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm quite confused going through the Emarsys scripting language documentation.

How do I reference products and contacts? In terms of products, where do I store the product data in Emarsys?

Thanks,

David V.

entry.title: Re: CR2020 viewer print output is incorrect when PrintMod...
entry.content:
Download page has a Printer test app:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

It has a routine to Set Printer and Select POC or P2P, use it to allow users to select and set printer:

Printing Crystal Reports in .NET

entry.title: Is downtime required to compress BW infocubes in the ASE database system
entry.content:
Hi team,

Is system downtime required when compressing an InfoCube.

Thanks.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Is downtime required to compress BW infocubes in the...
entry.content:
Hello,

Compressing an InfoCube does not require any business "downtime" for the system. This means that your operations can continue as usual without any interruptions.

However, we recommend performing this task during less busy hours, such as overnight or over the weekend. This is to ensure optimal system performance and to minimize any potential impact on your business operations.

Regards,

SAP Support

entry.title: How to develop forms with EC Mini-Master to Track Promotion process
entry.content: We are looking for ways to use Successfactors to move away from Adobe Forms for our internal Promotion process as to automate some of the processes as part of our promotion process.  Curious if/how anyone else has setup this up within Successfactors.  WE are not fully live with EC but we have a Mini-Master implementation.

entry.title: Re: Usage Statistics of Table and Canvas Reports to Plan Transition to Stories
entry.content: I exported the Site Statistics for 2024 data looks good with 36000 rows. I did the same for 2023 and my data is much less with 1277 rows. There were gaps of dates between usage data. I opened a ticket with SAP Support questioning if they purge old data. How do you get site statistics accurately if the data is not there? Anyone else experienced this?

entry.title: Re: How to filter data in SAP Build Apps using record collection
entry.content: @vikas_992001 How did you bind the Page variable which is list of two objects with a Record collection which is list of 11 objects? Any formula to use?

entry.title: Re: Still unable to pass DateTime parameters with "MS OLE...
entry.content: PLEASE test with ODBC, it's not the first time MS OLE DB client has had problems and works differently than ODBC, it may be something Microsoft needs to fix.
